steps: Go to https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys and log in or create a free Stripe account
You will see your Publishable key and Secret key listed
To use an existing secret key, click the 'Reveal live key' button next to the Secret key
To create a new restricted API key, click 'Create restricted key', name it, set permissions, and click 'Create key'
Copy the secret key (starts with 'sk_live_' for live or 'sk_test_' for test mode)
Use test mode keys (starting with 'sk_test_') for development

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Secret API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Stripe secret API key. Use sk_test_... for testing or sk_live_... for production.

notes: Free account available; Stripe charges per transaction (2.9% + 30 cents for card payments)
Use test mode keys (sk_test_...) during development — no real charges occur
Secret keys are shown only once when creating restricted keys; standard keys can be revealed in the dashboard
Consider using restricted keys with only the permissions your integration needs
Live mode keys are gated behind account activation (requires business verification)

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve account balance to verify the API key
